data.title: Arq Restructures CEO Compensation Toward Long-Term Equity Alignment
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Arq Inc ( (ARQ) ).

On July 23, 2026, Arq, Inc. amended the employment agreement of President and CEO Robert Rasmus, setting a new term through July 23, 2029, while reducing his annual salary to $50,000 and eliminating his eligibility for annual bonuses and long-term incentive plans, offset by reimbursement of certain business expenses. In connection with the amendment, the board’s compensation committee granted him 600,000 time-based RSUs and 600,000 performance-based RSUs under the 2026 Omnibus Incentive Plan, with vesting tied to tenure, stock-price milestones up to $9.00, and change-in-control or termination protections, signaling a greater alignment of his compensation with shareholder value.

On July 17, 2026, Arq and Rasmus also revised a prior inducement RSU award covering 400,000 units granted at his 2023 hire, extending the performance period for earning those shares from July 17, 2026 to July 17, 2029. The revised mix of low cash pay, extended equity horizons, and performance-based vesting is likely to reduce near-term cash outflows while incentivizing long-term stock performance, affecting how investors and other stakeholders view executive incentives and governance at the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (ARQ) stock is a Buy
with a $3.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Arq Inc stock,
see the ARQ Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ARQ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ARQ is a Neutral.

ARQ’s score is most constrained by weak profitability and negative free cash flow despite rapid revenue growth and low leverage. Technicals are moderately positive in the short term but remain weak versus longer-term moving averages. Guidance and operational commentary were constructive (reiterated 2026 targets and FCF expectations), while liquidity/cost pressures and uncertainty around GAC restart timing remain key risks.

To see Spark’s full report on ARQ stock,
click here.

More about Arq Inc

Arq, Inc. operates in the broader energy and industrial materials sector, focusing on technologies and products that convert reclaimed or alternative feedstocks into higher-value fuels or carbon materials. The company targets customers seeking lower-cost, lower-emission solutions, positioning itself as a player in the transition toward cleaner and more sustainable energy-related products.

Average Trading Volume: 350,589

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $88.86M

An announcement from Allurion Technologies ( (ALURD) ) is now available.

On July 17, 2026, Allurion Technologies, Inc. announced that Chief Executive Officer Shantanu K. Gaur, M.D. resigned from his role and from the company’s board of directors, with both departures effective immediately. The company stated that his resignation was not due to any disagreement regarding operations, policies or practices, and the board has not yet named an interim or permanent successor.

Following Dr. Gaur’s departure, Chief Operating Officer Ojas Buch has assumed responsibility for overseeing Allurion’s day-to-day operations, including duties previously handled by the CEO. Buch’s title and compensation remain unchanged, indicating a transitional leadership structure that relies on the existing executive team while the board determines the company’s longer-term CEO appointment.

with a $45.00 price target.

Organon ( (OGN) ) has shared an update.

On July 23, 2026, Organon held a virtual special meeting of stockholders to vote on a proposed merger under an agreement dated April 26, 2026 with Sun Pharmaceutical Holdings USA, Inc. and its subsidiary. The plan calls for Organon to be merged with a Sun Pharma subsidiary and to continue as a wholly owned subsidiary of Sun Pharma USA, signaling a shift in control and integration into Sun Pharma’s global operations.

Of the 262,609,433 shares outstanding as of the June 15, 2026 record date, 74.51% were represented, providing a quorum for the meeting. Stockholders overwhelmingly adopted the merger agreement and also approved, on a non-binding advisory basis, the compensation arrangements for Organon’s named executive officers related to the merger, clearing key shareholder hurdles and reducing execution risk for the transaction.

On July 22, 2026, Paymentus Holdings disclosed that director Adam Malinowski, originally nominated by private equity firm Accel-KKR, notified the board of his intention to resign, effective July 23, 2026, with the company stating his departure did not stem from any disagreement over its operations, policies or practices. On July 23, 2026, the board appointed Accel-KKR managing director Gregory Williams as an independent Class II director to fill the vacancy under existing stockholder nomination rights, a move that maintains Accel-KKR’s board representation while reinforcing Paymentus’s governance structure, though Williams will not receive standard outside director compensation and is not expected to join board committees.

The company noted that Williams brings extensive experience in corporate strategy, finance, transactions and technology investments, backed by a long tenure on private company boards and academic credentials from the University of Virginia’s Darden School and Harvard College. Paymentus will enter into its standard director and officer indemnification agreement with Williams and emphasized that he is not involved in any related-party transactions, signaling continuity in governance practices and oversight for shareholders despite the boardroom change.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

First Western Financial ( (MYFW) ) has issued an announcement.

First Western Financial reported second-quarter 2026 net income available to common shareholders of $5.7 million, or $0.57 per diluted share, down from $6.2 million in the first quarter of 2026 but more than double the $2.5 million earned in the second quarter of 2025. Net interest income rose 4.3% sequentially to $21.8 million, net interest margin improved to 2.90%, and average noninterest-bearing deposits increased 5.1% quarter over quarter, supporting higher book and tangible book value per share.

Management highlighted that second-quarter 2026 performance benefited from disciplined expense control, stable asset quality, and an improved mix of interest-earning assets and deposits, which lowered funding costs and boosted margins. While non-interest income declined due to weaker mortgage loan gains and lower risk management and insurance fees, revenue and profitability remained significantly higher than a year earlier, positioning the bank to leverage strong loan and deposit pipelines and sustain attractive returns for shareholders through 2026.

The company plans to discuss its second-quarter 2026 results with analysts and investors on July 24, 2026, via an investor call and webcast, providing further detail on financial performance and strategic trends. This communication underscores management’s emphasis on balance sheet optimization and relationship-based banking as key drivers of ongoing growth in a competitive regional banking environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (MYFW) stock is a Buy
with a $33.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on First Western Financial stock,

data.title: Shareholders Approve Charter Amendments and Extension Flexibility
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation Class A ( (FORL) ).

On June 22, 2026, Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation held a special meeting at which shareholders approved amendments to its charter and trust agreement, allowing the board to extend the deadline to complete an initial business combination up to twelve times, in one‑month increments, from June 22, 2026 to June 22, 2027 by funding $75,000 into the trust account for each extension. Shareholders also voted to remove the prior limitation that restricted redemptions based on net tangible asset levels and authorized meeting adjournments if needed, moves that provide the SPAC with greater flexibility to pursue a deal while significantly shrinking its public float after holders of 893,090 Class A shares, about 88 percent of the public shares, chose to redeem, leaving 121,427 Class A shares outstanding.

More about Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation Class A

Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation is a special purpose acquisition company focused on identifying and consummating an initial business combination with an operating business. The company raised capital through its March 22, 2023 initial public offering, with proceeds placed in a trust account managed by Continental Stock Transfer & Trust Company to fund a future transaction and potential shareholder redemptions.

data.title: Columbia Banking System Reports Strong Second-Quarter 2026 Results
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Columbia Banking System ( (COLB) ) has issued an update.

On July 23, 2026, Columbia Banking System, Inc. reported second-quarter 2026 results, posting net income of $208 million and operating net income of $217 million, with diluted earnings per share of $0.73 and operating diluted EPS of $0.76. Management highlighted that commercial loan growth, disciplined execution and ongoing balance sheet repositioning underpinned performance in a dynamic operating environment.

Quarterly net interest income slipped to $589 million and net interest margin edged down to 3.93%, partly due to interest income reversals, while non-interest income rose to $88 million and non-interest expense fell to $375 million as merger costs declined and acquisition-related savings materialized. Credit indicators remained contained, with annualized net charge-offs at 0.25% of average loans, provision expense of $27 million and non-performing assets at 0.42% of total assets, alongside strong capital ratios, a $0.37 per-share dividend paid on June 15, 2026, $199 million of stock repurchases and deposit inflows of about $1.25 billion from 2026 small business and retail deposit campaigns.

Columbia also reported improved operating efficiency, with its efficiency ratio down to 55.15% and operating efficiency ratio at 52.92%, as pre-provision net revenue and operating pre-provision net revenue increased to $302 million and $314 million, respectively. Returns on average assets and common equity rose versus the prior quarter, reinforcing management’s message that the bank’s diversified model, sound credit culture and capital generation are supporting sustainable shareholder value despite modest balance sheet deleveraging.

The most recent analyst rating on (COLB) stock is a Buy
with a $38.00 price target.

OBOOK Holdings, Inc. Class A ( (OWLS) ) has shared an update.

In a July 23, 2026 update, OwlTing Group reported that payment volume on OwlPay Harbor, its digital currency-based cross-border enterprise payments platform, grew more than fivefold quarter over quarter in Q2 2026. The company said monthly volume has increased every month since early 2026 and that July’s first three weeks had already surpassed June’s full-month volume.

Across the broader OwlPay platform, spanning fiat and digital currency transactions, cumulative processed payment volume exceeded US$600 million as of June 30, 2026, with 67 enterprise clients onboarded by that date. The business is seeing particular momentum in Asia-focused trade corridors and in regions such as the Middle East and Africa where U.S. dollar access is constrained, suggesting OwlTing’s regulated infrastructure is strengthening its competitive position in cross-border B2B payments and reinforcing its growth trajectory.

More about OBOOK Holdings, Inc. Class A

OwlTing Group, the operating brand of OBOOK Holdings Inc., is a global fintech company focused on building regulated cross-border payment infrastructure, blending fiat and digital currency rails. Its OwlPay platform serves enterprises across the U.S., Asia, Europe and other regions, leveraging licenses in 42 U.S. states, a VASP registration in Poland and a Bank API license in Japan to move funds into harder-to-reach markets.

Founded in Taiwan, the company runs subsidiaries in markets including the United States, Japan, Poland, Singapore, Hong Kong, Thailand and Malaysia, targeting business clients that need faster, cheaper international payments. An announcement from Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A ( (ZBAO) ) is now available.

On July 23, 2026, Zhibao Technology Inc. reported that it has elected to follow certain Cayman Islands home country corporate governance practices instead of specified Nasdaq rules, including those requiring a majority-independent board, regular meetings of independent directors, annual shareholder meetings, and shareholder approvals for major equity issuances and compensation plans. The company disclosed that its Cayman counsel, Ogier (Cayman) LLP, confirmed these practices are permissible under Cayman law and the firm’s governing documents, signaling a governance framework that departs in several respects from typical U.S. domestic standards while otherwise remaining broadly aligned with Nasdaq requirements, with implications for how shareholder rights and board oversight are structured.

Valuation is also not a clear positive, as the negative P/E reflects current losses and no dividend yield is available to offset risk.

click here.

More about Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A

Zhibao Technology Inc., a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market, operates as a foreign private issuer subject to Nasdaq’s corporate governance listing standards. The company follows corporate governance practices permitted under Cayman Islands law and its memorandum and articles of association, while selectively using exemptions available to non-U.S. issuers.

An announcement from Banco BBVA Argentina ( (BBAR) ) is now available.

On July 23, 2026, Banco BBVA Argentina announced it would pay the third and final installment of its 2026 cash dividend, totaling ARS 23,003,675,041, as previously approved at the shareholders’ meeting on April 28, 2026 and authorized by the Argentine central bank in May. The payment, equivalent to ARS 37.5441433549 per share and representing 3,754.4143354952% of the bank’s capital stock, will be made on August 6, 2026 to shareholders of record as of August 3, 2026, with local payments handled via Caja de Valores and ADS holders paid by The Bank of New York Mellon under applicable market rules.

The bank specified that only holdings registered on the record date for each installment would qualify for the declared dividend, underscoring the importance of accurate registry for investors. It also detailed that applicable tax withholdings, including amounts for the 2025 Personal Property Tax where relevant and a 7% income tax withholding, will be deducted from distributions, shaping the net cash return to shareholders and signaling continued adherence to Argentine tax and regulatory requirements.

data.title: Eagle Financial Reports Q2 Results Amid Credit Provisions
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Eagle Financial Services ( (EFSI) ) has provided an announcement.

On July 23, 2026, Eagle Financial Services reported second-quarter 2026 results that showed consolidated net income of $5.0 million, up from $3.7 million in the prior quarter but down from $5.3 million a year earlier, alongside a quarterly cash dividend of $0.31 per share payable on August 14, 2026. The quarter was marked by the sale of its membership interest in Bearing Insurance Group, which generated a $3.5 million pre-tax gain and boosted noninterest income to $8.6 million, but adjusted net income fell to $2.2 million as higher provisions for credit losses weighed on underlying earnings despite solid loan growth, a 2.74% increase in net loans, and an improved net interest margin of 3.86%, supporting management’s view that balance sheet actions over the past two years have strengthened capital, liquidity and profitability.

For the quarter ending June 30, 2026, adjusted net income declined 40.5% from the March quarter and 57.7% from the year-ago period, reflecting the drag from higher credit loss provisioning even as loan interest income rose 5.0% on 3.0% average loan growth and a modestly higher loan yield. Total interest expense fell versus both the prior quarter and prior year, aided by lower costs on Federal Home Loan Bank advances that were fully paid off in March, while annualized returns on average equity and assets held at 10.35% and 1.08%, respectively, underscoring a franchise that remains operationally sound but more cautious on credit, with implications for shareholders who benefit from ongoing dividends and a more resilient balance sheet.

data.title: Vireo Growth expands footprint with Cannabist asset acquisition
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Vireo Growth ( (TSE:VREO) ).

On July 19, 2026, Vireo Health of Arcadia, a subsidiary of Vireo Growth, signed a purchase agreement to acquire equity interests and assets from Columbia Care and affiliated Cannabist entities that run cannabis businesses in Colorado, Illinois, Massachusetts, New Jersey and West Virginia. The deal is structured around up to US$18.75 million in cash and up to US$16.25 million in seller financing, and is being implemented through Cannabist’s Canadian court-supervised restructuring.

Closings are expected to occur market by market through 2026 and into 2027, subject to cannabis regulatory approvals and a sale order from the Ontario Superior Court of Justice. The transaction will deepen Vireo’s Colorado presence and add four new state markets, potentially lifting its pro forma footprint to about 230 dispensaries across 15 states and positioning it among the largest U.S. cannabis retailers, while Cannabist advances its strategic review and court-led restructuring.

More about Vireo Growth

Vireo Growth Inc. is a vertically integrated U.S. cannabis company operating cultivation, manufacturing, retail dispensaries, home delivery, distribution and agricultural supply businesses. With operations in 10 states and more than 170 dispensaries, it targets both cannabis and adjacent agricultural markets, using strategic acquisitions and disciplined capital allocation to scale its platform and build consumer brands.

Spar Group ( (SGRP) ) has provided an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, SPAR Group, Inc. announced that its common stock will be delisted from the Nasdaq Stock Market and begin trading on the OTCQB Venture Market at the open on July 23, 2026, retaining the ticker symbol SGRP. The company emphasized that this change in listing venue does not alter its daily operations, commitment to customers, employees and partners, or its status as a reporting company with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Management framed the transition as neutral to the firm’s core business activities, noting that SPAR’s shares will remain eligible for electronic trading through broker-dealers once OTC quotations are available. While shareholders are not required to take any action, the move from a major exchange to the OTCQB may affect the stock’s visibility and liquidity, with potential implications for investor perception and access to capital over time.

data.title: EVI Industries Advances Sudsies Acquisition and Financing Structure
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

EVI Industries ( (EVI) ) just unveiled an update.

On July 17, 2026, EVI Industries entered into a series of definitive agreements under which its subsidiaries will acquire substantially all assets and certain liabilities of Miami-based Sudsies, Inc. and affiliated entities, plus the personal goodwill of founder Jason Loeb, for aggregate consideration exceeding $34 million in cash and EVI common stock. The transactions, structured across four asset and goodwill purchase agreements with escrow and customary indemnity provisions, are expected to close within 30 to 45 days, subject to conditions including simultaneous completion of all transactions by September 1, 2026.

The Sudsies deal marks EVI’s first dedicated expansion into the consumer garment care services market, a multibillion-dollar, recurring-demand segment that the company plans to pursue through a new division while maintaining its core focus on commercial laundry distribution and service. Sudsies, founded in 1996 and positioned as a premier South Florida garment care operator with strong revenue growth and profitability, is expected to become the platform for a national consumer garment care business, with Loeb continuing to lead the brand, and management indicating the acquisition should be accretive to earnings in the fiscal year ending June 30, 2027.

On July 20, 2026, EVI disclosed that a press release announcing the Sudsies agreements had inadvertently overstated Sudsies’ five-year EBITDA compound annual growth rate as 62%, and corrected this figure to 31%. The company also noted that the stock components of the Sudsies-related transactions will be issued privately to accredited investors Rudski and Loeb under exemptions from Securities Act registration, underscoring a financing structure that avoids public equity issuance for this expansion.

The most recent analyst rating on (EVI) stock is a Buy
with a $32.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on EVI Industries stock,
see the EVI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on EVI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, EVI is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mixed financial performance: strong revenue growth and stable gross margin are offset by thinner profitability, rising leverage, and weaker, less consistent free cash flow. Technical signals are bearish with the price below key moving averages and a negative MACD, while valuation is a headwind due to a high P/E and no dividend yield support.

To see Spark’s full report on EVI stock,
click here.

More about EVI Industries

EVI Industries, Inc. is a value-added distributor and provider of advisory and technical services focused on commercial laundry operations. Through its subsidiaries, the company sells and leases commercial laundry equipment and related parts, and offers planning, design, installation, maintenance, and repair services to retail, commercial, industrial, institutional, and government customers.

Under a long-term buy-and-build strategy launched in 2015, EVI has grown from a single Miami-based distributor into North America’s leading commercial laundry distribution and service enterprise. The group now encompasses dozens of businesses and hundreds of sales and service professionals, and continues to target both organic growth and acquisitions within the laundry sector.

data.title: SL Green Realty posts wider loss, raises 2026 guidance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from SL Green Realty ( (SLG) ) is now available.

SL Green Realty reported a larger net loss for the quarter and six months ended June 30, 2026, with diluted EPS of ($0.38) in the second quarter versus ($0.16) a year earlier, while funds from operations came in at $1.43 per share, below the prior-year period that was boosted by one-time mortgage income. Despite softer year-on-year FFO, same-store cash NOI in Manhattan rose and office occupancy climbed to 94.7%, supported by robust leasing activity with significant rent mark-to-market gains.

The company executed a series of capital recycling moves in May 2026, selling the residential and retail portions of 7 Dey Street and a 49% joint venture interest in 346 Madison Avenue, and entering a contract to sell 10 East 53rd Street, collectively generating substantial cash proceeds earmarked partly for debt repayment. SL Green also continued to deploy capital through its $1.3 billion opportunistic debt fund and repurchased $14.1 million of common stock, while raising its 2026 FFO and net income guidance, signaling management’s confidence in improved operating income, incremental fee revenue and higher contributions from One Vanderbilt Avenue.

The most recent analyst rating on (SLG) stock is a Sell
with a $38.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SL Green Realty stock,
see the SLG Stock Forecast page.

SLG scores in the mid-range primarily due to weak profitability and elevated leverage despite improving revenue/margins and solid free cash flow. The company also operates an opportunistic debt fund and engages in joint venture developments, positioning itself as a major player in New York City’s commercial office market.

Its portfolio strategy combines core office holdings, targeted property sales and structured debt investments to optimize cash flow and capital recycling. SL Green’s emphasis on Manhattan same-store performance, active leasing and partnerships with global developers underpins its role as a bellwether for the health of the city’s high-end office sector.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

T Stamp, Inc. Class A ( (IDAI) ) just unveiled an update.

On July 17, 2026, T Stamp, Inc. held a shareholder conference call to review recent business developments, highlighting that a six-year contract extension signed in 2025 with an S&P 500 bank remains the foundation of its U.S. financial services business. The company expects approximately $2.8 million in gross revenue from this relationship in 2026 and $3 million in 2027, driven in part by the bank’s migration to its low-code orchestration layer, and it now has over 100 financial institutions, plus a major life insurer and other enterprises, using that platform, including its first credit union adopting a standalone AMVA product.

Management detailed growing opportunities in Africa, including work with the continent’s largest telecom operator that is projected to generate at least $2 million in revenue by the end of 2027 and could scale to $3 million to $5 million in annual recurring revenue, as well as a pipeline of government-related contracts in Ghana and Nigeria that the company conservatively discounts to $4.6 million for the balance of 2026 and 2027. The call also underscored the strategic importance of its new Tokyo office, where partnerships with Japanese firms such as Digital Platformer, INTEC, and SEIKO Systems have advanced pilot testing and planned 2026 rollouts across multiple identity and authentication use cases, which management believes will enhance Trust Stamp’s credibility and accelerate its expansion in the Japanese market.

The most recent analyst rating on (IDAI) stock is a Buy
with a $8.00 price target.

T Stamp, Inc., also known as Trust Stamp, operates in digital identity and authentication technologies serving financial services, telecoms, governments, and enterprises. Its offerings include a low-code orchestration layer and specialized products such as AMVA, supporting banks, insurers, healthcare, fintech firms, and emerging sovereign technology use cases across the U.S., Africa, and Japan.

The company’s operations span Atlanta and Malta headquarters with an 80-plus person team across four continents, and it is increasingly positioning itself around Sovereign-AI and related sovereign technologies.
